stories.headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
stories.summary: Humanoid robots completed a half-marathon in Beijing faster than human participants, demonstrating significant progress in robotic speed and endurance capabilities. The event highlights how robotics technology has advanced to the point where machines can now outpace humans in athletic competitions designed for people.

stories.headline: Bulgarians head to polls for eighth time in five years
stories.summary: Bulgaria holds its eighth parliamentary election in five years, driven by persistent political instability and recurring failures to form stable governments. Pro-Russian former president Radev leads in polling, reflecting voter frustration with corruption and demand for change despite the exhaustion of repeated electoral cycles.

stories.headline: Investigators examine whether Ukraine terrorist attack was directed by Russia
stories.summary: A gunman killed six people during a shooting rampage in Kyiv before police fatally shot him to end the incident. Ukrainian investigators are now determining whether Russia directed or coordinated the attack as part of its broader conflict with Ukraine.

stories.headline: Pope Leo plays down rift with Trump, doubles down on peace
stories.summary: Pope Leo characterizes his recent tensions with Trump as overblown and expresses reluctance to engage in public dispute, stating it serves neither's interests. The pontiff reiterates his commitment to pursuing peace initiatives despite their policy disagreements.

stories.headline: Chief Rabbi says attacks 'gathering momentum' after new synagogue arson attempt
stories.summary: UK counterterrorism police are investigating an attempted arson attack at a synagogue in north-west London, adding to a pattern of recent incidents against Jewish sites. The Chief Rabbi has stated that such attacks are accelerating, raising concerns about the security of Jewish institutions in the country.

stories.headline: Bodies of 50 infants dumped at Trinidad graveyard
stories.summary: Authorities in Trinidad and Tobago discover dozens of remains, primarily infants, buried in an unmarked section of a cemetery, prompting investigations into how the bodies came to be there. The discovery raises questions about burial practices, record-keeping at the facility, and whether foul play or negligence played a role in the unauthorized interments.

stories.headline: Starmer would have blocked Mandelson over vetting failure, ministers say
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government states he would have blocked Peter Mandelson's appointment as EU envoy had he known about the diplomat's failed security vetting process. The revelation creates tension within the government as Mandelson faces potential parliamentary questioning that could contradict Starmer's account of the appointment.

stories.headline: North Korea launches ballistic missiles towards sea off its east coast
stories.summary: North Korea launches several ballistic missiles into the sea east of the peninsula, continuing a pattern of weapons testing that has accelerated over recent months. The launches occur as tensions remain elevated on the Korean peninsula and amid broader regional instability involving Iran.

stories.headline: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: Ukraine's president objects to the United States extending exemptions that allow Russian oil sales to continue despite international sanctions. The waiver permits transactions that generate substantial revenue for Russia's war effort while limiting the economic pressure on Moscow.
